Imports

../../support/http

Types

BodyStream = object
Represents a stream for reading the request body in chunks. 
OnRequest = proc (req: var Request) {.nimcall.}
Callback type for handling HTTP requests. 
Request = object
  httpMethod*: EvhttpCmdType ## The HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.).
  raw*: ptr evhttp_request   ## The underlying evhttp_request pointer.
  path*: string              ## The request path.
  uri*: Uri                  ## The parsed URI object.
  uriQuery*: Option[TableRef[string, string]] ## Lazily initialized query parameters from the URI.
  headers*: Option[HttpHeaders] ## The request headers (lazily initialized).
  body*: Option[string]      ## The request body (lazily initialized).
  routeParams*: TableRef[string, string] ## The route parameters extracted from the URL.
Represents an HTTP request. 
StartupCallback = proc () {....gcsafe.}
Callback type for server startup (optional). 
WebServer = ref object
  base*: ptr event_base      ## The underlying event base.
  httpServer*: ptr evhttp    ## The underlying evhttp server.
  port*: Port                ## The port the server listens on.
Represents an HTTP server.

Procs

proc addCallback(server: WebServer; path: string; callback: proc (
    req: ptr evhttp_request; arg: pointer) {.cdecl.}) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Adds a callback for a specific path. 
proc addEvent(server: WebServer;
              callback: proc (fd: cint; events: cshort; arg: pointer) {.cdecl.}) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Adds a custom event to the server's event loop. 
proc addHeader(req: var Request; name, value: string) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Sets a header in the request. This uses the lower-level C API. Note: This does overwrite existing caches in req.headers. 
proc clearHeaders(req: var Request) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Clears all headers from the request. This uses the lower-level C API. Note: This also clears the cached headers table. 
proc drainChunk(stream: var BodyStream; len: int) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Removes len bytes from the buffer (after processing a peeked chunk). 
proc endChunk(req: Request) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Ends a chunked HTTP response. 
proc findHeader(req: Request; name: string): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Use the lower-level C API to check if a header exists. This is more efficient than fetching all headers. This does not update any cached headers in req.headers. 
proc getBody(req: var Request): Option[string] {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Retrieves the body from Request Lazily initializes the body on first access. Then caches it for future accesses. 
proc getBodyStream(req: var Request): BodyStream {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns a BodyStream for reading the request body in chunks. 
proc getHeader(req: var Request; key: string): Option[string] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns return a header from Request 
proc getHeaders(req: var Request): Option[HttpHeaders] {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Retrieves the headers from the HTTP request as a Table. Lazily initializes the headers on first access. Then caches them for future accesses. 
proc getIp(req: var Request): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Retrieves the IP address from request 
proc getMethod(req: var Request): HttpMethod {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Maps the EvhttpCmdType to a more user-friendly HttpMethod enum. To avoid the mapping overhead use req.httpMethod directly. 
proc getOutputHeaders(req: Request): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc getQuery(req: var Request): Option[TableRef[string, string]] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the parsed query parameters from the request URI as a Table. Lazily initializes on first access. Then caches them for future accesses. 
proc newWebServer(port: Port = Port(8080)): WebServer {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Creates a new WebServer instance. 
proc peekChunk(stream: BodyStream; maxLen: int): (pointer, int) {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns a pointer and length to the next available chunk (up to maxLen). No copy is performed. The pointer is only valid until the next buffer operation. 
proc readChunk(stream: var BodyStream; maxLen: int): string {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Reads up to maxLen bytes from the stream. Returns empty string when no more data is available. 
proc removeHeader(req: var Request; name: string) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Removes a header from the request. This uses the lower-level C API. Note: This does update existing caches in req.headers. 
proc runServer(onRequest: OnRequest; startupCallback: StartupCallback;
               port = Port(3000)) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                                    forbids: [].}
Starts the HTTP server using pkg/libevent and runs the request handler. 
proc send(req: Request; code: HttpCode; body: string;
          httpHeaders: HttpHeaders = nil) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Sends an HTTP response using HttpCode enum. 
proc send(req: Request; code: int; body: string; httpHeaders: HttpHeaders = nil) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc sendChunk(req: Request; data: string) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Sends a chunked HTTP response. 
proc sendFile(req: Request; bytes: seq[uint8]; resHeaders: HttpHeaders) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Sends a byte sequence as a file response. Note: This is not zero-copy and is suitable for smaller files. 
proc sendFile(req: Request; filePath: string; resHeaders: HttpHeaders) {.
    ...raises: [OSError], tags: [ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
Sends a file as a single chunk using zero-copy. Note: Does not check for file type. Ensure to set the correct Content-Type header. 
proc start(server: var WebServer) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Start the web server with default no-op request handler. 
proc start(server: var WebServer; onRequest: OnRequest;
           startupCallback: StartupCallback = nil) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Starts the web server event loop. 
proc startChunk(req: Request) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Starts a chunked HTTP response. 
proc startPartialChunk(req: Request) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Starts a partial content chunked HTTP response. Indicates that the response is for a partial content request (HTTP 206). 
proc streamFile(req: var Request; filePath: string;
                resHeaders: HttpHeaders = nil) {.
    ...raises: [OSError, KeyError, ValueError], tags: [ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}

Streams a file over HTTP using chunked transfer encoding and zero-copy file segments.

Supports HTTP Range requests for partial content delivery.

This method is suitable for large files as it streams them in chunks.

For smaller files consider using sendFile which sends the entire file in one go.

Ensure to set the correct contentType for the file being served as it does not check the file type.

Templates

template withChunks(reqInstance: Request; httpHeaders: HttpHeaders; body) {.
    inject.}
Enables chunked transfer encoding for the response. This template wraps the body of code that sends chunks. Once the body of code that sends chunks. Once the body is done executing it finalizes the chunked response.

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-03-08 02:58:24 UTC
